reasoning: Based on the empirical base rate, any-increase ACLED questions start at roughly 0.225. The 360-day trailing average as of mid-October 2025 was high (20.167 events per 30 days), likely front-loaded by huge post-election unrest in late 2024 and early 2025. However, there are major reports from October 2025 detailing a massive, unprecedented resurgence of the Cabo Delgado insurgency. UN OCHA released multiple Situation Reports in October, and news reports state that 'for the first time since hostilities began, all 17 districts of Cabo Delgado have been directly affected.' This explosive geographic expansion of the conflict means widespread non-violent territory transfers, mass looting of abandoned villages, and desperate troop deployments by Mozambican and Rwandan forces — all of which fall squarely under ACLED's 'Strategic developments' code. Therefore, the 30-day period leading into early November 2025 (covering October) is highly likely to feature an intense spike in such events, increasing the chance of beating the 20.167-event threshold. As we look to the later resolution dates in November and January, the initial surge of tactical movements and lootings may stabilize, meaning rates will likely regress toward the mean (and potentially below the elevated 360-day average), resulting in probabilities moving closer to the base rate over time.
